name: research.deepseek
description: Ask DeepSeek directly for public, non-sensitive research and drafts of skills, plans, outlines, or code snippets. DeepSeek never receives private context and never writes files.

message.description: A public, non-sensitive research or drafting prompt for DeepSeek.

purpose: Use one low-cost DeepSeek call for everyday public research and advisory drafting while structurally refusing PRIVATE and SECRET content before the provider is called.
whenToUse: Osman explicitly asks to use or ask DeepSeek for a non-sensitive question.
Osman wants public research, comparison, brainstorming, or an outline from a low-cost model.
Osman wants DeepSeek to draft a skill, implementation plan, or code snippet without writing files.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for I-9, E-Verify, finance, payroll, email content, SSNs, EINs, credentials, PRIVATE data, or SECRET data.
Do not use to read repo files automatically or to create, edit, commit, push, or deploy files.
Do not use when the user did not request DeepSeek and the normal domestic model route is appropriate.
requiredContext: The exact public, non-sensitive question or drafting request to send to DeepSeek.
Any public source text deliberately included by the user, without automatic memory or repo context.
missingContextQuestions: What exact non-sensitive question should DeepSeek answer?
If a repo file is needed, which specific public, secret-free file are you deliberately opting in?

outputContract.format: Direct DeepSeek research or draft, followed by a short advisory-only boundary when a write would be needed.
mustInclude: The requested research, plan, skill draft, outline, or code snippet.
A clear handoff to the approval-gated build path for any durable write.
mustAvoid: Sending or repeating PRIVATE or SECRET content.
Claiming files were read, created, edited, committed, pushed, or deployed.

safetyRules: Run the shared classifier before resolving or calling the DeepSeek provider.
Refuse PRIVATE and SECRET content before any DeepSeek network call and direct it to Groq/Ollama.
Pass only the explicit user prompt; never attach memory or repo files automatically.
Keep all outputs advisory and route durable writes through existing approvals.
approvalRequiredFor: Committing, pushing, opening a PR, or deploying
positiveExamples: Ask DeepSeek to compare two public TypeScript validation strategies.
Use DeepSeek to research current approaches to agent evaluation.
Have DeepSeek draft a SKILL.md outline for a public API workflow.
Ask DeepSeek for a plan to refactor this non-sensitive sample function.
Use DeepSeek to draft a code snippet for parsing public JSON data.
negativeExamples: Ask DeepSeek to review this employee's I-9 and SSN.
Send DeepSeek the pasted recruiter email from my inbox.
Have DeepSeek edit the repo, commit the files, and push them.

evaluationPrompts.input: Ask DeepSeek to research public benchmarks for TypeScript runtimes.
Use DeepSeek to draft a skill for summarizing public release notes.
Have DeepSeek outline a plan for this non-sensitive API client.
Ask DeepSeek for a JavaScript snippet that parses a public RSS feed.
Use the cheap DeepSeek research tool to compare two public libraries.
Ask DeepSeek to review this I-9 record and SSN 123-45-6789.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous safety case: explicit DeepSeek request must reach the structural refusal gate.
Ambiguous provider choice with no explicit DeepSeek signal.

evaluationPrompts.input: Check my inbox for recruiter messages.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Private email task belongs to Iris and the domestic route.

evaluationPrompts.input: Use DeepSeek to draft the plan, then implement and commit it.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Multi-skill request: DeepSeek may draft, while the approval-gated builder owns writes.
